What you post outlives the mood you posted it in.
The internet remembers longer than you regret.
A screen makes it easy to forget there's a person on the other end.
Anonymity lowers the cost of cruelty. It shouldn't lower your standards.
Before you send it, imagine reading it back a year from now.
Convenience is not the same as consent.
Every account you create is a door. Lock the ones you're not using.
Speed is the enemy of good judgment online.
You are not just a user. You are a participant in whatever you share.
The best security habit is the one you don't have to think about anymore.
If you wouldn't say it to their face, don't say it to their feed.
Curiosity is healthy. Clicking without thinking is not the same thing.
Your data has a shelf life longer than your memory of sharing it.
A private life online is built one small boundary at a time.
Being online longer doesn't mean being online better.
